title: Persistência e invariantes locais da Fase 4

artifacts.reference: 60 passed with clean PostgreSQL 16 ephemeral; 26 passed and 34 skipped without database
0012_work_persistence; nine work/storage tables; partial unique active-reservation index
SANDBOX task funding debits=1000 and credits=1000
TaskFunded, PaidTaskPublished, AssignmentReserved and SubmissionCreated observed; AssignmentExpired covered by integration test

positive_results: Company, PaidTask, TaskFundingReservation, funding lines, Assignment, AssignmentReservation, StoredObject, SubmissionDraft and Submission survive service restart.
Row locking and a PostgreSQL partial unique index allow only one ACTIVE reservation for a task.
The expiry worker is idempotent, emits AssignmentExpired and never updates TaskFundingReservation or its ledger transaction.
The HTTP flow covers admin creation/funding/publication, participant evidence, eligible listing, reservation, draft save, quarantine and final submission.
Submission responses omit private content and reject another participant or a quarantined object.
The registered web runtime fingerprint remained unchanged during Phase 4.
limitations: Self-verified evidence in a dirty workspace does not satisfy the S2 acceptance gate.
No real object bytes were sent to a private bucket and no malware scanner produced a signed/read-back verdict.
SANDBOX funding does not prove availability of real company, matching or realized-bonus balances.
The admin identity remains a configured Nostr pubkey allowlist, not a separately deployed identity provider.
DIV-FLOW-001 requires an explicit product decision before connecting the Figma publish-opportunity wizard to a financial aggregate.
This evidence authorizes no external effect.
